The takeaway: the money is increasingly flowing to creators below the macro tier, but the creators in that tier still don't have the tools to capture it efficiently. Brand deals are the largest revenue stream, and they're the one most creators are least equipped to pursue. The creator economy isn't slowing down. It's broadening. And the infrastructure to support that broadening is the next decade of work. [Likes: 6, Comments: 1]; Andrew Lukas (2mo): Killed one of my favorite ideas this weekend. Since day one of Trovio, we'd built a deliberate pause into our top of funnel. A friction point. A moment where the product slowed down, gathered context from the user, and came back with a real plan. The logic felt [...] already up. The lesson isn't "friction is bad." Friction in the right place absolutely works. The lesson is that the story I was telling myself about why users would value that pause was a story, not a finding. The customers and the data were telling me something different, and I had to listen. Onto the next thing the data is trying to tell us! [Likes: 18, Comments: 0]; Trovio (2mo): If you've ever opened a brand email and frozen because you don't know what to send back, this is for you. Most creators lose deals at the response, not the pitch. The brand reaches out. You think "I should send a media kit." You don't have one. You start making one in Canva. Three days pass. The brand has moved on. Pitch Kit fixes the thing in the middle. Media kit built from your real content in under a [...] That's a stock photo site with people in it. The entire industry is built around making creators findable by brands. Nobody is building software that makes creators fundable by themselves. Tools that help you pitch. Tools that help you negotiate. Tools that help you understand your own value before someone else assigns it to you. The 1% with agents already have this. Humans doing it manually, taking 15-20% off the top. The other 99% get a profile in someone else's database. We're building for the 99%. Scaling is not about chasing bigger numbers — it’s about maintaining performance as you grow. Follow the data, increase budgets methodically. [...] Lately, I’ve been diving deep into the creator economy. The more I learn, the more obvious it becomes: being a full-time creator making a living wage is still a statistical anomaly. 🤯 Despite projections that the space will balloon to $528B (!!) by 2030, only 4% of creators will clear six figures, and more than half won’t even make $15k a year. Crazy right? Believe it or not, the barrier isn’t audience growth – it’s converting followers into predictable income. Brands now expect super detailed audience insights, while platforms hoard the most valuable data. And if you’re a creator chasing sponsorships through affiliate networks, it’s getting harder to stand out in a flood of AI content (especially if you want to keep those engagement and conversion metrics high). At Trovio, we’re building [...] What Growth Feels Like In 2026 From the outside, it looks like the same game—brands posting, creators hustling, startups running ads (albeit everyone is using the same AI visuals lol) But I can tell from my 40k monthly ad-spend and straight up client insights - The easy wins are gone. Organic reach that used to stretch for weeks now burns out in a FEW hours. Literally. The entire world is flooding the "internet" (is the internet the version where the humans browse or the ai agents browse? I'm not even sure anymore) with this AI-driven content (otherwise known as 'slop').. Paid campaigns that once scaled effortlessly hit invisible ceilings. results.title: Trovio - A Digital Agent for Creators
results.content:
Trovio hero illustration

# ADigital Talent Agent for Creators

Trovio is the agent for every creator, whether you're at 5K or 500K followers. Brand matches, pitch drafts, and a media kit brands actually open.

Get Your Digital Agent

## What You Post Matters More Than How Many Follow You.

What you post becomes a media kit. The themes become brand matches. Your voice becomes the pitch.

### The media kit that doesn't need a designer.

Pick a handle, pick a color, hit generate. Trovio builds your shareable media kit from the content you already make.

### Matched to what you post, not who follows you.

20 to 30 brands, grouped by the theme they'd actually fit. Three tiers: obvious fit, related overlap, and unexpected-but-defensible.

### Pitches in your own voice, anchored to real posts. [...] Your content does the heavy lifting

Your agent reads what you post, identifies the themes you're already known for, and uses those as the foundation for everything it does — brand matches, content strategy, outreach. You don't tell us your niche. We read it.

Personalized to your creator journey

Your agent learns your style, your audience, and your trajectory. The match we surface at 8K isn't the match we surface at 80K. Everything evolves with you.

Anchored to what actually worked

Every recommendation, every pitch, every match is anchored to real posts and real signals from your audience. No vague advice. No generic template with your name dropped in.

Your voice, not a template [...] Drafting pitches in your voice

Media kitLive

From here, it works with you: matching you with brands that fit what you post, drafting outreach in your voice anchored to your best post, and packaging your story into a media kit brands open. It only gets sharper as you grow.

Get Your Digital Agent

## Our Creators

### How a creator like Maya sends her first brand pitch in an afternoon

## charging after cancellation

I even contacted my bank to block the transactions and whatever automated system they are using they are bypassing with different ways to attempt to charge. I have all my receipts of cancellation and all of that stuff and I’ve reached out to customer support for the past two weeks and they don’t respond.
